page: Microsoft Azure F16ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Fadsv7 family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, featuring 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core allocation. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes 1890 GB of local NVMe storage. It operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark memory scores are rated as strong (top 25%). However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth (both read and write for large blocks) fall within the average (middle 50%) range. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds on small 135M models, though performance scales down to average levels for medium and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F16ads_v7 offers a balanced resource density by pairing dedicated high-frequency AMD cores with a substantial 1890 GB local disk. This combination makes it suitable for workloads requiring fast single-threaded execution alongside local storage caching. Recommended workloads include relational databases, web serving, software compilation, and moderate data processing tasks that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and low-latency local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure F16ads_v7 is an x86_64 server instance from the Fadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. It features 16 physical cores, 16 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by stress-ng and gzip compression benchmarks ranking in the top 10%. While multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks align with the average tier, its single-threaded decompression and small-model LLM text generation are exceptionally strong. The combination of dedicated high-frequency cores, a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and substantial local storage makes this server well-suited for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose workloads requiring reliable single-core execution and local disk caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16ads_v7 is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16ads_v7 is a 16-vCPU server with an AMD EPYC 4.5 GHz processor, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD-powered instance featuring high-frequency single-core execution, balanced memory, and substantial local storage for database and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 cores running at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory and 1890 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression benchmark performance
Average-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
Suitable for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose applications
